description: Rigid block (RBlock) object management in PFC. RBlocks are polyhedral rigid bodies used in discrete element simulations. They support full 3D kinematics including position, orientation (Euler angles), velocity, spin, forces, and moments.

functions.name: containing
functions.description: Find the rigid block containing the point. The core shapes are used to determine if the point falls within the rigid block.

functions.name: energies
functions.description: Get the rblock total energy contribution as a dictionary with string keys and float values.

functions.name: energy
functions.description: Get the rblock total energy contribution. This is the sum over all rblocks of the energy with name energy_na,e (see the set energy command). Available energies are: “energy-body”, “energy-damp” and “energy-kinetic”.

functions.name: find
functions.description: Get the RBlock object with the given ID number.

functions.name: inbox
functions.signature: itasca.rblock.inbox(lower_bound: vec, upper_bound: vec, intersect=True) -> Tuple of RBlock objects
functions.description: Get a tuple of rblocks with extents intersecting a box. The extent is the axis-aligned bounding box of the rblock. The if the optional keyword argument intersect is False only rblocks with positions falling in the box are returned.

functions.name: near
functions.description: Find the closest rblock to a point. If the optional keyword argument radius is non-zero, only search within this radius.
